children.text: Condemnation proceedings; purchase without condemnation; acceptance of donations of land
The Secretary of the Interior is authorized to cause condemnation proceedings to be instituted in the name of the United States under the provisions of

children.tail: , to acquire title to the lands, interests therein, or rights pertaining thereto within the said battlefield, and the United States shall be entitled to immediate possession upon the filing of the petition in condemnation in the United States District Court for the District of Maryland:

, That when the owner of such lands, interests therein, or rights pertaining thereto shall fix a price for the same, which, in the opinion of the Secretary of the Interior, shall be reasonable, the Secretary may purchase the same without further delay:
, That the Secretary of the Interior is authorized to accept, on behalf of the United States, donations of lands, interests therein, or rights pertaining thereto required for the battlefield:
, That title and evidence of title to lands and interests therein acquired for said battlefield shall be satisfactory to the Secretary of the Interior.
” substituted in text for “the Act of
, entitled ‘An Act to authorize condemnation of lands for sites for public buildings and for other purposes’ (25 Stat.L. 357)” on authority of
, the first section of which enacted Title 40, Public Buildings, Property, and Works.
substituted “battlefield” for “Monocacy National Military Park” wherever appearing.

children.text: “Battlefield” substituted in text for “park” in view of redesignation of Monocacy National Military Park as Monocacy National Battlefield by
